run_name: NATP Module 4: Prevention and Management of Catastrophes and Unusual Occurrences — Source-First Dry Run

learner_facing_title: NATP Module 4: Prevention and Management of Catastrophes and Unusual Occurrences
purpose: The purpose of this unit is to introduce the student to the concepts and procedures related to the patient's/resident's safety including environmental emergency issues The Nurse Assistant `s role in creating a safe environment for the patient/resident is discussed.

objectives.objective_title: Describe general rules for providing a safe environment for the Nurse Assistant
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Describe general rules for providing a safe environment for the Nurse Assistant
objectives.source_excerpt: A. Lecture/Discussion A. Written test Describe general rules for providing a safe environment for the B. Show examples of B. Reports safety issues Nurse Assistant. C. Follows policy to protect A. Use body mechanic principles incident form B. Use ergonomics C. Discuss TQI and self from injury C. Know the policy and procedures regarding OSHA safety laws on performance A. Written test use of equipment and handling hazardous materials (MSDS) improvement issues B. Responds to mock D. Wipe up spills immediately and identify wet floors with the A. Lecture/Discussion safety issue according to appropriate signs B. Show pictures of policy E. Walk, never run in halls and watch carefully at intersections F. Use contents of containers only if they have proper labels/dates hazards and have students identify them and you know how to use them correctly and describe how to G. Tag and report broken equipment report/correct hazard H. Report unsafe situations C. Observe student in I. Use 3-pronged plugs on electrical equipment J. Refuse to do any task that you do not know how to do K. Know proper operation of equipment L. Watch linen and garbage cans for safety hazards (sharps) M. Report if sharps container is over half full N. Know procedure to follow in case of personal injury 1. Report the injury immediately to the supervisor 2. Fill out an incident report/unusual occurrence form as per facility policy and procedure 3. Seek medical help as necessary O. Patient/resident and staff safety is an important quality assurance issue

objectives.objective_title: Describe general rules for providing a safe environment for the patient/resident
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Describe general rules for providing a safe environment for the patient/resident
objectives.source_excerpt: Describe general rules for providing a safe environment for the patient/resident. A. Know the National Patient Safety Goals B. Check wrist bands/name tags before performing any task on a patient/resident C. Use side rails when appropriate and know facility policy and procedure on side rails D. Have the patient/resident use handrails/appropriate assistive device when unstable Module 4: Prevention and Management of Catastrophes and Unusual Occurrences E. Have patient/resident wear non-skid footwear when ambulatory patient/resident room F. Place the call signal light within the reach of the Ask if it is a safe environment and if not patient/resident and instruct them on correct use how to correct it G. Lock wheels on bed/gurney/wheelchair when transferring the patient/resident H. Answer call lights promptly I. Use night-lights to help ensure good lighting and reduce obstacle hazards J. Keep the bed in the lowest position except when tending to the patient/resident K. Check bed/chair alarms ensuring working condition L. Keep environment clutter free

objectives.source_excerpt: A. Lecture/Discussion A. Written test Describe two of the top safety issues for B. Shows videos for use of B. Uses assistive devices patients/residents and interventions to prevent them. A. Falls are 70% of all patient/resident-related accidents. Most devices according to policy C. Demonstrate use of C. Reports and corrects falls occur during 4:00pm and 8:00pm and during shift changes B. Ways to prevent falls devices including gait fall hazards belt and heating pads D. Monitors temperature 1. Proper position in bed/wheelchair and readjust every two D. Review transfer and hours ambulation techniques of liquids used for E. Demonstrate use of soft patients/residents 2. Soft protective devices as ordered and necessary restraints and avoids exposure 3. Side rails as necessary and use caution when raising and F. Show examples of of patient/resident equipment hazards E. Uses heating pads lowering Watch for arms/legs & tubing. Make sure the rails G. Show pictures of various according to policy are locked in the up position types of burns 4. Brakes should be on when transferring patient/resident to or from wheelchair/bed/gurney 5. Comfort items (water, call bell, urinal, etc.) should be easy for patient/resident to reach 6. Answer call lights promptly 7. Areas should have good lighting and be free of clutter 8. Check to ensure foot latches on beds and foot supports on the wheelchair are out of the way to prevent tripping or hitting staff or patient's/resident's legs 9. Wipe up spills promptly 10. Meet the comfort needs of the patient/resident promptly (water and elimination) Module

objectives.objective_title: List the safety rules to be followed for the patient/resident receiving oxygen therapy
objectives.exact_objective_statement: List the safety rules to be followed for the patient/resident receiving oxygen therapy
objectives.source_excerpt: List the safety rules to be followed for the patient/resident receiving oxygen therapy. A. Oxygen therapy A. Lecture/Discussion A. Written test 1. Oxygen is abbreviated O2 and measured in liters per minute B. Show Video on oxygen B. Observes safety (L/min) use guidelines when caring 2. A colorless, odorless, tasteless gas is essential for respiration C. Show examples of for patient/resident with (breathing) oxygen equipment like oxygen 3. Some patients/residents need supplemental oxygen to assist nasal cannula, masks, them in breathing oxygen tank, etc. D. Demonstrate placement 4. Oxygen is supplied in portable tanks, through wall outlets or of oxygen devices concentrators E. Demonstrate oxygen tank on/off procedure 5. Oxygen increases the risk of fire since it supports combustion and is one of the three elements needed for fire B. When oxygen is in use, specific safety precautions need to be followed 1. Place a "no smoking, oxygen in use" sign on the door of patient's/resident's room as well as over their bed 2. Smoking is never allowed near the oxygen by anyone, including the patient/resident or visitor 3. Keep oxygen-tubing open and free of kinks 4. Check electrical equipment use in presence of oxygen (electric razors, fans and radios) Electrical items must be turned off before unplugging 5. No flammable liquids, such as alcohol, nail polish remover or petroleum based products (Chap Stick, Vaseline) 6. Watch for static when combing patient's/resident's hair, using wool blankets, or nylon materials in clothing, etc. C. Types of oxygen equipment 1. Green metal portable tan

objectives.objective_title: Discuss the legal and psychological implication of the use of postural supports
objectives.exact_objective_statement: Discuss the legal and psychological implication of the use of postural supports
objectives.source_excerpt: A. Lecture/Discussion A. Written test Discuss the legal and psychological implications of the use of B. Review patient/resident B. Observes patient rights postural supports. A. Legal implications of the use of postural supports rights when using postural supports, protective 1. Must have a doctor's order to use protective devices including devices and restraints stated reason for use, type of device, and for how long 2. Must have informed consent 3. Patient/resident rights require that patient/resident not be restrained unnecessarily or for the convenience of the staff 4. Unnecessary restraint of a patient/resident could constitute false imprisonment Ask nurse for explanation of needs if in doubt B. Psychological implication of the use of postural supports 1. Explain to the patient/resident and the family the reason for using protective devices 2. The patient/resident is usually aware of restricted movement and may struggle against the supports 3. Reassure and support the patient/resident and the family Module 4: Prevention and Management of Catastrophes and Unusual Occurrences
objectives.weight_reason: Deterministic source-density allocation from source recommended theory hours; assessment/clinical/certificate time excluded.

objectives.source_excerpt: Describe the overview of bioterrorism. A. Define terrorism A. Lecture/Discussion B. Possible terrorist acts B. Handout 412 � 1. Biological threat Homeland Security a. Bacteria � Anthrax Advisory System b. Viruses � Smallpox C. Ask students to view c. Toxins � Ricin information on US 2. Chemical threat Department of 3. Explosions Homeland Security 4. Nuclear blast website 5. Radiation threat http://wwwdhsgov/natio C. Possible types of bioterrorism threats nal-terrorism-advisory- 1. Explosion system 2. Chemical/Biological Attack 3. Nuclear/Radiological Attack D. National Terrorism Advisory System (NTAS) replaces the Homeland Security Advisory System (HSAS) that was color- coded 1. Imminent Threat 2. Elevated Threat Alert E. Sunset Provision � expires after a specific time period Module 4: Prevention and Management of Catastrophes and Unusual Occurrences
